1	"""Pure contracts describing what a speculative training algorithm consumes.
2	
3	This module intentionally contains no factories, model classes, or runtime
4	objects.  Algorithm implementations live behind provider ports; deployment and
5	transport are resolved by the application layer.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import re
11	from dataclasses import dataclass, fields, is_dataclass
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import FrozenSet, Iterable, Tuple
14	
15	_ALGORITHM_NAME = re.compile(r"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$")
16	
17	
18	def _normalized_names(
19	    values: Iterable[str],
20	    *,
21	    field_name: str,
22	    allow_empty: bool = False,
23	) -> FrozenSet[str]:
24	    if isinstance(values, str):
25	        raise TypeError(f"{field_name} must be an iterable of names, not a string")
26	    normalized = frozenset(values)
27	    if not normalized and not allow_empty:
28	        raise ValueError(f"{field_name} must not be empty")
29	    invalid = [
30	        repr(value)
31	        for value in normalized
32	        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value or value.strip() != value
33	    ]
34	    if invalid:
35	        raise ValueError(
36	            f"{field_name} must contain non-empty names without surrounding "
37	            f"whitespace, got {', '.join(sorted(invalid))}"
38	        )
39	    return normalized
40	
41	
42	def _assert_pure_value(value: object, *, path: str) -> None:
43	    """Reject executable or opaque state recursively from public contracts."""
44	
45	    if isinstance(value, type) or callable(value):
46	        raise TypeError(f"{path} must be a pure value, got executable {value!r}")
47	    if value is None or isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool, Enum)):
48	        return
49	    if isinstance(value, (tuple, list, set, frozenset)):
50	        for index, item in enumerate(value):
51	            _assert_pure_value(item, path=f"{path}[{index}]")
52	        return
53	    if isinstance(value, dict):
54	        for key, item in value.items():
55	            _assert_pure_value(key, path=f"{path}.key")
56	            _assert_pure_value(item, path=f"{path}[{key!r}]")
57	        return
58	    if is_dataclass(value) and not isinstance(value, type):
59	        for field in fields(value):
60	            _assert_pure_value(
61	                getattr(value, field.name),
62	                path=f"{path}.{field.name}",
63	            )
64	        return
65	    raise TypeError(
66	        f"{path} must contain only serializable contract values, got "
67	        f"{type(value).__name__}"
68	    )
69	
70	
71	class FeatureMode(str, Enum):
72	    """How algorithm-ready target features are consumed."""
73	
74	    OFFLINE = "offline"
75	    STREAMING = "streaming"
76	
77	
78	@dataclass(frozen=True)
79	class DraftRequirement:
80	    """Pure compatibility requirements for a draft architecture.
81	
82	    Draft configuration loading, target-derived generation, validation, and
83	    model construction belong to the draft-model registry and algorithm-owned
84	    providers.  Only stable identifiers and declarative override names belong
85	    here.
86	    """
87	
88	    compatible_architectures: FrozenSet[str]
89	    default_architecture: str
90	    supported_overrides: FrozenSet[str] = frozenset()
91	    fixed_override_values: Tuple[Tuple[str, int], ...] = ()
92	
93	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
94	        architectures = _normalized_names(
95	            self.compatible_architectures,
96	            field_name="compatible_architectures",
97	        )
98	        overrides = _normalized_names(
99	            self.supported_overrides,
100	            field_name="supported_overrides",
101	            allow_empty=True,
102	        )
103	        if self.default_architecture not in architectures:
104	            raise ValueError(
105	                "default_architecture must be present in " "compatible_architectures"
106	            )
107	        fixed_values = tuple(self.fixed_override_values)
108	        fixed_names = [name for name, _value in fixed_values]
109	        duplicates = sorted(
110	            name for name in set(fixed_names) if fixed_names.count(name) > 1
111	        )
112	        if duplicates:
113	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate fixed override values: {duplicates}")
114	        unsupported = sorted(set(fixed_names) - overrides)
115	        if unsupported:
116	            raise ValueError(
117	                "fixed_override_values must name supported overrides, got "
118	                f"{unsupported}"
119	            )
120	        if any(
121	            not isinstance(name, str)
122	            or isinstance(value, bool)
123	            or not isinstance(value, int)
124	            for name, value in fixed_values
125	        ):
126	            raise TypeError("fixed_override_values must contain (name, integer) pairs")
127	        object.__setattr__(self, "compatible_architectures", architectures)
128	        object.__setattr__(self, "supported_overrides", overrides)
129	        object.__setattr__(self, "fixed_override_values", fixed_values)
130	
131	
132	@dataclass(frozen=True)
133	class OfflineStorageContract:
134	    """Raw offline record shape before an algorithm normalizer is applied."""
135	
136	    format: str
137	    required_tensors: FrozenSet[str]
138	    normalizer: str
139	    schema_version: int = 1
140	
141	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
142	        for field_name in ("format", "normalizer"):
143	            value = getattr(self, field_name)
144	            if not isinstance(value, str) or not value or value.strip() != value:
145	                raise ValueError(
146	                    f"{field_name} must be a non-empty name without surrounding "
147	                    "whitespace"
148	                )
149	        if self.schema_version != 1:
150	            raise ValueError("only offline storage schema_version=1 is supported")
151	        object.__setattr__(
152	            self,
153	            "required_tensors",
154	            _normalized_names(
155	                self.required_tensors,
156	                field_name="required_tensors",
157	            ),
158	        )
159	
160	
161	@dataclass(frozen=True)
162	class FeatureContract:
163	    """Algorithm-ready tensors for one ``(mode, modality)`` pair."""
164	
165	    mode: FeatureMode
166	    modality: str
167	    required_tensors: FrozenSet[str]
168	    optional_tensors: FrozenSet[str] = frozenset()
169	    allowed_target_representations: FrozenSet[str] = frozenset()
170	    default_target_representation: str | None = None
171	    schema_version: int = 1
172	    storage: OfflineStorageContract | None = None
173	
174	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
175	        try:
176	            mode = FeatureMode(self.mode)
177	        except ValueError as exc:
178	            raise ValueError(f"unsupported feature mode {self.mode!r}") from exc
179	        if (
180	            not isinstance(self.modality, str)
181	            or not self.modality
182	            or self.modality.strip() != self.modality
183	        ):
184	            raise ValueError(
185	                "modality must be a non-empty name without surrounding whitespace"
186	            )
187	        if self.schema_version != 1:
188	            raise ValueError("only feature schema_version=1 is supported")
189	        required = _normalized_names(
190	            self.required_tensors,
191	            field_name="required_tensors",
192	        )
193	        optional = _normalized_names(
194	            self.optional_tensors,
195	            field_name="optional_tensors",
196	            allow_empty=True,
197	        )
198	        representations = _normalized_names(
199	            self.allowed_target_representations,
200	            field_name="allowed_target_representations",
201	            allow_empty=True,
202	        )
203	        overlap = required & optional
204	        if overlap:
205	            raise ValueError(
206	                "required_tensors and optional_tensors must be disjoint, got "
207	                f"{sorted(overlap)}"
208	            )
209	        if representations:
210	            if self.default_target_representation not in representations:
211	                raise ValueError(
212	                    "default_target_representation must be present in "
213	                    "allowed_target_representations"
214	                )
215	        elif self.default_target_representation is not None:
216	            raise ValueError(
217	                "default_target_representation requires at least one allowed "
218	                "target representation"
219	            )
220	        if mode is FeatureMode.OFFLINE and self.storage is None:
221	            raise ValueError("offline feature contracts require a storage contract")
222	        if mode is FeatureMode.STREAMING and self.storage is not None:
223	            raise ValueError("streaming feature contracts cannot define storage")
224	        object.__setattr__(self, "mode", mode)
225	        object.__setattr__(self, "required_tensors", required)
226	        object.__setattr__(self, "optional_tensors", optional)
227	        object.__setattr__(self, "allowed_target_representations", representations)
228	
229	    @property
230	    def key(self) -> tuple[FeatureMode, str]:
231	        return self.mode, self.modality
232	
233	
234	@dataclass(frozen=True)
235	class AlgorithmCapabilities:
236	    """Static algorithm constraints independent of deployment topology."""
237	
238	    attention_backends: FrozenSet[str]
239	    required_batch_size: int | None = None
240	    supports_compact_teacher: bool = False
241	    supports_vocab_mapping: bool = False
242	    allows_aux_layer_override: bool = False
243	
244	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
245	        attention_backends = _normalized_names(
246	            self.attention_backends,
247	            field_name="attention_backends",
248	        )
249	        if self.required_batch_size is not None and (
250	            isinstance(self.required_batch_size, bool)
251	            or not isinstance(self.required_batch_size, int)
252	            or self.required_batch_size <= 0
253	        ):
254	            raise ValueError("required_batch_size must be a positive integer or None")
255	        for field_name in (
256	            "supports_compact_teacher",
257	            "supports_vocab_mapping",
258	            "allows_aux_layer_override",
259	        ):
260	            if not isinstance(getattr(self, field_name), bool):
261	                raise TypeError(f"{field_name} must be a bool")
262	        object.__setattr__(self, "attention_backends", attention_backends)
263	
264	
265	@dataclass(frozen=True)
266	class AlgorithmSpec:
267	    """Complete topology-free, executable-free contract for one algorithm."""
268	
269	    name: str
270	    draft: DraftRequirement
271	    feature_contracts: Tuple[FeatureContract, ...]
272	    capabilities: AlgorithmCapabilities
273	
274	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
275	        if not isinstance(self.name, str) or not _ALGORITHM_NAME.fullmatch(self.name):
276	            raise ValueError(
277	                "algorithm name must start with a lowercase letter and contain "
278	                "only lowercase letters, digits, '_' or '-'"
279	            )
280	        if not isinstance(self.draft, DraftRequirement):
281	            raise TypeError("draft must be a DraftRequirement")
282	        if not isinstance(self.capabilities, AlgorithmCapabilities):
283	            raise TypeError("capabilities must be AlgorithmCapabilities")
284	        contracts = tuple(self.feature_contracts)
285	        if not contracts:
286	            raise ValueError("feature_contracts must not be empty")
287	        invalid = [
288	            type(contract).__name__
289	            for contract in contracts
290	            if not isinstance(contract, FeatureContract)
291	        ]
292	        if invalid:
293	            raise TypeError(
294	                "feature_contracts must contain FeatureContract values, got "
295	                f"{invalid}"
296	            )
297	        keys = [contract.key for contract in contracts]
298	        duplicates = sorted(
299	            (mode.value, modality)
300	            for mode, modality in set(keys)
301	            if keys.count((mode, modality)) > 1
302	        )
303	        if duplicates:
304	            raise ValueError(
305	                "feature_contracts contains duplicate (mode, modality) keys: "
306	                f"{duplicates}"
307	            )
308	        object.__setattr__(self, "feature_contracts", contracts)
309	        _assert_pure_value(self, path="AlgorithmSpec")
310	
311	    @property
312	    def modalities(self) -> FrozenSet[str]:
313	        return frozenset(contract.modality for contract in self.feature_contracts)
314	
315	    @property
316	    def feature_modes(self) -> FrozenSet[FeatureMode]:
317	        return frozenset(contract.mode for contract in self.feature_contracts)
318	
319	    @property
320	    def supports_online(self) -> bool:
321	        """Whether any streaming feature contract is declared."""
322	
323	        return FeatureMode.STREAMING in self.feature_modes
324	
325	    def supports(self, mode: FeatureMode | str, modality: str) -> bool:
326	        try:
327	            resolved_mode = FeatureMode(mode)
328	        except ValueError:
329	            return False
330	        return any(
331	            contract.key == (resolved_mode, modality)
332	            for contract in self.feature_contracts
333	        )
334	
335	    def feature_contract(
336	        self,
337	        mode: FeatureMode | str,
338	        modality: str,
339	    ) -> FeatureContract:
340	        try:
341	            resolved_mode = FeatureMode(mode)
342	        except ValueError as exc:
343	            raise KeyError(f"unknown feature mode {mode!r}") from exc
344	        for contract in self.feature_contracts:
345	            if contract.key == (resolved_mode, modality):
346	                return contract
347	        supported = sorted(
348	            (contract.mode.value, contract.modality)
349	            for contract in self.feature_contracts
350	        )
351	        raise KeyError(
352	            f"algorithm {self.name!r} does not support "
353	            f"({resolved_mode.value!r}, {modality!r}); supported: {supported}"
354	        )
355	
356	
357	__all__ = [
358	    "AlgorithmCapabilities",
359	    "AlgorithmSpec",
360	    "DraftRequirement",
361	    "FeatureContract",
362	    "FeatureMode",
363	    "OfflineStorageContract",
364	]

"""Explicit immutable catalog of SpecForge's built-in algorithms."""

from __future__ import annotations

from specforge.algorithms.dflash.providers import create_registration as dflash
from specforge.algorithms.domino.providers import create_registration as domino
from specforge.algorithms.dspark.providers import create_registration as dspark
from specforge.algorithms.eagle3.providers import create_registration as eagle3
from specforge.algorithms.peagle.providers import create_registration as peagle
from specforge.algorithms.registry import AlgorithmRegistry


def builtin_algorithm_registry() -> AlgorithmRegistry:
    """Return a fresh immutable catalog without module-level mutation."""

    return AlgorithmRegistry((eagle3(), peagle(), dflash(), domino(), dspark()))


__all__ = ["builtin_algorithm_registry"]

9	"""DraftTrainStrategy: per-draft-model required features + forward/loss + projection.
10	
11	A strategy is the only place that knows how a draft model (EAGLE3 / P-EAGLE /
12	DFlash / Domino) turns a normalized ``TrainBatch`` into a loss; ``TrainerCore``
13	stays branch-free and the strategy owns the target projection. Imports model
14	code, so it is imported by training entry points, not at package load.
15	"""
16	
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	import abc
20	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
21	from typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Tuple
22	
23	import torch
24	import torch.nn as nn
25	
26	from specforge.runtime.contracts import TrainBatch
27	
28	
29	@dataclass(frozen=True)
30	class StepOutput:
31	    """Per-step result: loss + strategy-specific metrics, kept generic so
32	    per-position (TTT) and single-scalar strategies share one trainer loop."""
33	
34	    loss: torch.Tensor
35	    metrics: Dict[str, Any]
36	    ratio_metrics: Dict[str, Tuple[Any, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)
37	
38	
39	@dataclass(frozen=True)
40	class StepContext:
41	    """Training-schedule state passed into ``forward_loss`` for objectives that
42	    depend on where in training we are (e.g. Domino's decaying ``lambda_base``);
43	    most strategies ignore it."""
44	
45	    global_step: int = 0
46	    total_steps: Optional[int] = None
47	
48	
49	def linear_lambda_base(
50	    global_step: int,
51	    total_steps: int,
52	    lambda_start: float = 1.0,
53	    decay_ratio: float = 0.5,
54	) -> float:
55	    """Domino base-loss weight: linear decay from ``lambda_start`` to 0 over the
56	    first ``total_steps * decay_ratio`` steps, then 0, clamped to ``[0, 1]``.
57	    Requires a real ``total_steps`` (> 0)."""
58	    decay_steps = max(1, int(total_steps * decay_ratio))
59	    progress = min(global_step / decay_steps, 1.0)
60	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, lambda_start * (1.0 - progress)))
61	
62	
63	class DraftTrainStrategy(abc.ABC):
64	    name: str
65	    required_features: set
66	
67	    @abc.abstractmethod
68	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
69	        """The module whose parameters the optimizer/backend owns."""
70	
71	    def validate_batch(self, batch: TrainBatch) -> None:
72	        missing = {f for f in self.required_features if f not in batch.tensors}
73	        if missing:
74	            raise ValueError(
75	                f"{self.name} batch missing required features {sorted(missing)}; "
76	                f"present={sorted(batch.tensors)}"
77	            )
78	
79	    @abc.abstractmethod
80	    def forward_loss(
81	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional["StepContext"] = None
82	    ) -> StepOutput: ...
83	
84	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
85	        """Select the keys this strategy persists as draft weights."""
86	        return state_dict
87	
88	
89	def _prepare_eagle_target(
90	    *,
91	    target_head: Optional[nn.Module],
92	    target_repr: Optional[str],
93	    input_ids: torch.Tensor,
94	    target: torch.Tensor,
95	    loss_mask: torch.Tensor,
96	    device: torch.device,
97	) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
98	    """Normalize EAGLE-family teacher features for a training forward.
99	
100	    Online capture already shifts logits and input IDs. Offline capture stores
101	    the target model's final hidden state, so the frozen target head owns the
102	    equivalent shift and projection to full-vocabulary logits.
103	    """
104	    if target_repr == "hidden_state":
105	        if target_head is None:
106	            raise ValueError(
107	                "target_repr='hidden_state' requires a target_head to re-run "
108	                "the lm_head projection"
109	            )
110	        input_ids, target, loss_mask = target_head.preprocess(
111	            input_ids, target, loss_mask
112	        )
113	        target = target_head(target.to(device))
114	        return input_ids.to(device), target, loss_mask.to(device)
115	    return input_ids.to(device), target.to(device), loss_mask.to(device)
116	
117	
118	class Eagle3TrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
119	    """EAGLE3 TTT strategy wrapping the existing ``OnlineEagle3Model``.
120	
121	    For ``target_repr == "hidden_state"`` the strategy re-runs the frozen
122	    ``TargetHead`` over the stored target hidden state; ``logits`` /
123	    ``pruned_logits`` are used as delivered. The ``t2d`` vocab map is applied
124	    inside ``OnlineEagle3Model.forward``.
125	    """
126	
127	    name = "eagle3"
128	    required_features = {
129	        "input_ids",
130	        "attention_mask",
131	        "loss_mask",
132	        "hidden_state",
133	        "target",
134	    }
135	
136	    def __init__(
137	        self,
138	        eagle3_model: nn.Module,
139	        *,
140	        target_head: Optional[nn.Module] = None,
141	        ploss_decay: float = 0.8,
142	        compact_teacher: bool = False,
143	        compact_teacher_chunk_size: Optional[int] = None,
144	    ) -> None:
145	        self.eagle3_model = eagle3_model
146	        self.target_head = target_head
147	        self.ploss_decay = ploss_decay
148	        self.compact_teacher = compact_teacher
149	        self.compact_teacher_chunk_size = compact_teacher_chunk_size
150	        if compact_teacher:
151	            self._validate_compact_teacher()
152	
153	    def _validate_compact_teacher(self) -> None:
154	        """Validate the offline compact-teacher contract before the first step.
155	
156	        The strategy is constructed after the vocab mapping has been loaded and
157	        after FSDP wrapping.  FSDP exposes the wrapped module as ``module``;
158	        walking that one boundary keeps validation independent of the backend
159	        while the actual forward still goes through the wrapped model.
160	        """
161	        if self.target_head is None:
162	            raise ValueError(
163	                "compact teacher requires the offline target_head; it is not "
164	                "available for online capture"
165	            )
166	
167	        model = self.eagle3_model
168	        if not hasattr(model, "draft_model") and hasattr(model, "module"):
169	            model = model.module
170	        draft_model = getattr(model, "draft_model", None)
171	        if draft_model is None:
172	            raise ValueError(
173	                "compact teacher requires an EAGLE3 model with a draft_model"
174	            )
175	
176	        from specforge.core.compact_teacher import (
177	            validate_compact_teacher_enabled,
178	            validate_vocab_mapping_consistency,
179	        )
180	
181	        target_head_weight = getattr(
182	            getattr(self.target_head, "fc", None), "weight", None
183	        )
184	        vocab_size = (
185	            int(target_head_weight.shape[0])
186	            if target_head_weight is not None and target_head_weight.dim() >= 1
187	            else int(
188	                getattr(getattr(self.target_head, "config", None), "vocab_size", 0)
189	            )
190	        )
191	        draft_vocab_size = int(
192	            getattr(
193	                getattr(draft_model, "config", None),
194	                "draft_vocab_size",
195	                int(draft_model.t2d.sum().item()),
196	            )
197	        )
198	        validate_compact_teacher_enabled(
199	            is_online=False,
200	            draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
201	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
202	            t2d=draft_model.t2d,
203	            target_head_weight=target_head_weight,
204	            chunk_size=self.compact_teacher_chunk_size,
205	        )
206	        validate_vocab_mapping_consistency(draft_model.t2d, draft_model.d2t)
207	
208	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
209	        return self.eagle3_model
210	
211	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
212	        return next(self.eagle3_model.parameters()).device
213	
214	    def _prepare_target(
215	        self,
216	        target_repr: Optional[str],
217	        input_ids: torch.Tensor,
218	        target: torch.Tensor,
219	        loss_mask: torch.Tensor,
220	        device: torch.device,
221	    ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
222	        return _prepare_eagle_target(
223	            target_head=self.target_head,
224	            target_repr=target_repr,
225	            input_ids=input_ids,
226	            target=target,
227	            loss_mask=loss_mask,
228	            device=device,
229	        )
230	
231	    def forward_loss(
232	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
233	    ) -> StepOutput:
234	        self.validate_batch(batch)
235	        t = batch.tensors
236	        device = self._device()
237	        target_repr = batch.metadata.get("target_repr")
238	
239	        compact_kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = {}
240	        if self.compact_teacher:
241	            if target_repr != "hidden_state":
242	                raise ValueError(
243	                    "compact teacher is offline-only and requires "
244	                    "target_repr='hidden_state'"
245	                )
246	            # Preserve TargetHead.preprocess's shift exactly, but do not call
247	            # TargetHead.forward: OnlineEagle3Model streams the frozen head in
248	            # vocabulary chunks from these kwargs instead.
249	            input_ids, target_hidden, loss_mask = self.target_head.preprocess(
250	                t["input_ids"], t["target"], t["loss_mask"]
251	            )
252	            input_ids = input_ids.to(device)
253	            target_hidden = target_hidden.to(device)
254	            loss_mask = loss_mask.to(device)
255	            from specforge.core.compact_teacher import build_offline_teacher_inputs
256	
257	            target, compact_kwargs = build_offline_teacher_inputs(
258	                compact=True,
259	                target_model=self.target_head,
260	                target_hidden=target_hidden,
261	                chunk_size_arg=self.compact_teacher_chunk_size,
262	            )
263	        else:
264	            input_ids, target, loss_mask = self._prepare_target(
265	                target_repr, t["input_ids"], t["target"], t["loss_mask"], device
266	            )
267	        position_ids = t.get("position_ids")
268	        (
269	            plosses,
270	            acceptance_rates,
271	            acces,
272	            acc_corrects,
273	            acc_denoms,
274	            metric_losses,
275	            metric_loss_denoms,
276	        ) = self.eagle3_model(
277	            input_ids=input_ids,
278	            attention_mask=t["attention_mask"].to(device),
279	            loss_mask=loss_mask,
280	            target=target,
281	            hidden_states=t["hidden_state"].to(device),
282	            position_ids=position_ids.to(device) if position_ids is not None else None,
283	            **compact_kwargs,
284	        )
285	        weights = [self.ploss_decay**i for i in range(len(plosses))]
286	        loss = sum(weights[i] * plosses[i] for i in range(len(plosses)))
287	        return StepOutput(
288	            loss=loss,
289	            metrics={
290	                "plosses": [p.detach() for p in plosses],
291	                "acces": [a.detach() for a in acces],
292	                "acceptance_rates": [a.detach() for a in acceptance_rates],
293	                "acc_corrects": [c.detach() for c in acc_corrects],
294	                "acc_denoms": [d.detach() for d in acc_denoms],
295	                "metric_losses": [m.detach() for m in metric_losses],
296	                "metric_loss_denoms": [d.detach() for d in metric_loss_denoms],
297	            },
298	        )
299	
300	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
301	        # The target-copied embedding is skipped only when actually frozen; a
302	        # trainable embedding must be persisted (checked on the live module —
303	        # state_dict tensors are detached and carry no requires_grad).
304	        embed_frozen = all(
305	            not p.requires_grad
306	            for n, p in self.eagle3_model.named_parameters()
307	            if "embed" in n.lower()
308	        )
309	        return {
310	            k.replace("draft_model.", ""): v
311	            for k, v in state_dict.items()
312	            if "draft_model." in k and not (embed_frozen and "embed" in k.lower())
313	        }
314	
315	
316	class PEagleTrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
317	    """P-EAGLE COD strategy wrapping ``OnlinePEagleModel``.
318	
319	    P-EAGLE consumes the same target capture as EAGLE3. The runtime schema uses
320	    the singular ``hidden_state`` name, while ``OnlinePEagleModel.forward`` uses
321	    ``hidden_states``; this strategy is the explicit boundary between them.
322	    """
323	
324	    name = "peagle"
325	    required_features = {
326	        "input_ids",
327	        "attention_mask",
328	        "loss_mask",
329	        "hidden_state",
330	        "target",
331	    }
332	
333	    def __init__(
334	        self,
335	        peagle_model: nn.Module,
336	        *,
337	        target_head: Optional[nn.Module] = None,
338	    ) -> None:
339	        self.peagle_model = peagle_model
340	        self.target_head = target_head
341	
342	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
343	        return self.peagle_model
344	
345	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
346	        return next(self.peagle_model.parameters()).device
347	
348	    def forward_loss(
349	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
350	    ) -> StepOutput:
351	        self.validate_batch(batch)
352	        tensors = batch.tensors
353	        device = self._device()
354	        input_ids, target, loss_mask = _prepare_eagle_target(
355	            target_head=self.target_head,
356	            target_repr=batch.metadata.get("target_repr"),
357	            input_ids=tensors["input_ids"],
358	            target=tensors["target"],
359	            loss_mask=tensors["loss_mask"],
360	            device=device,
361	        )
362	
363	        lengths = tensors.get("lengths")
364	        if lengths is None:
365	            # P-EAGLE is currently batch-size 1. Deriving the document length
366	            # from the padding mask prevents COD samples from treating padded
367	            # positions as part of the document in the offline path.
368	            lengths = tensors["attention_mask"].sum(dim=-1)
369	
370	        loss, model_metrics = self.peagle_model(
371	            input_ids=input_ids,
372	            attention_mask=tensors["attention_mask"].to(device),
373	            loss_mask=loss_mask,
374	            target=target,
375	            hidden_states=tensors["hidden_state"].to(device),
376	            lengths=lengths.to(device),
377	        )
378	        if not isinstance(loss, torch.Tensor) or loss.numel() != 1:
379	            raise ValueError(
380	                "peagle model must return a scalar loss tensor; "
381	                f"got {type(loss).__name__} with shape="
382	                f"{getattr(loss, 'shape', None)}"
383	            )
384	
385	        metrics = {
386	            name: value.detach() if isinstance(value, torch.Tensor) else value
387	            for name, value in model_metrics.items()
388	        }
389	        correct = metrics.get("full_acc_sum")
390	        denominator = metrics.get("full_acc_total")
391	        if correct is not None and denominator is not None:
392	            correct = torch.as_tensor(correct, device=device)
393	            denominator = torch.as_tensor(denominator, device=device)
394	            metrics["accuracy"] = correct / denominator.clamp_min(1)
395	
396	        return StepOutput(loss=loss.reshape(()), metrics=metrics)
397	
398	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
399	        # Unlike the stock EAGLE3 path, P-EAGLE trains its token embeddings and
400	        # mask_hidden parameter. Persist the complete draft model so both survive
401	        # checkpoint/resume and export.
402	        return {
403	            key.replace("draft_model.", ""): value
404	            for key, value in state_dict.items()
405	            if "draft_model." in key
406	        }
407	
408	
409	class DFlashTrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
410	    """DFlash block-parallel strategy wrapping the existing ``OnlineDFlashModel``.
411	
412	    Shares the trainer/backend/loader/checkpoint spine with EAGLE3; only the
413	    per-step forward/loss differs (single block-wise pass, scalar loss, hard
414	    real-token labels — no target distribution, no vocab map). ``hidden_states``
415	    is DFlash's own schema name, distinct from EAGLE3's ``hidden_state``.
416	    """
417	
418	    name = "dflash"
419	    required_features = {"input_ids", "hidden_states", "loss_mask"}
420	
421	    def __init__(self, dflash_model: nn.Module) -> None:
422	        self.dflash_model = dflash_model
423	
424	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
425	        return self.dflash_model
426	
427	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
428	        return next(self.dflash_model.parameters()).device
429	
430	    def forward_loss(
431	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
432	    ) -> StepOutput:
433	        self.validate_batch(batch)
434	        t = batch.tensors
435	        device = self._device()
436	        loss, accuracy, model_metrics = self.dflash_model(
437	            input_ids=t["input_ids"].to(device),
438	            hidden_states=t["hidden_states"].to(device),
439	            loss_mask=t["loss_mask"].to(device),
440	        )
441	        metrics = {"accuracy": accuracy.detach()}
442	        if "accuracy_denom" in model_metrics:
443	            metrics["accuracy_denom"] = model_metrics["accuracy_denom"]
444	        return StepOutput(loss=loss, metrics=metrics)
445	
446	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
447	        # Everything trainable lives under draft_model.; the target
448	        # embedding/head are a separate module, not persisted as draft weights.
449	        return {
450	            k.replace("draft_model.", ""): v
451	            for k, v in state_dict.items()
452	            if "draft_model." in k
453	        }
454	
455	
456	class DSparkTrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
457	    """DSpark strategy over DFlash with target hidden-state supervision."""
458	
459	    name = "dspark"
460	    required_features = {
461	        "input_ids",
462	        "hidden_states",
463	        "loss_mask",
464	        "target_last_hidden_states",
465	    }
466	
467	    def __init__(self, dspark_model: nn.Module) -> None:
468	        self.dspark_model = dspark_model
469	
470	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
471	        return self.dspark_model
472	
473	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
474	        return next(self.dspark_model.parameters()).device
475	
476	    def forward_loss(
477	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
478	    ) -> StepOutput:
479	        self.validate_batch(batch)
480	        t = batch.tensors
481	        device = self._device()
482	        loss, accuracy, model_metrics = self.dspark_model(
483	            input_ids=t["input_ids"].to(device),
484	            hidden_states=t["hidden_states"].to(device),
485	            loss_mask=t["loss_mask"].to(device),
486	            target_last_hidden_states=t["target_last_hidden_states"].to(device),
487	        )
488	        metrics = {
489	            "accuracy": accuracy.detach(),
490	        }
491	        ratio_metrics = model_metrics.get("ratio_metrics", {})
492	        for name in (
493	            "accuracy_denom",
494	            "ce_loss",
495	            "l1_loss",
496	            "confidence_loss",
497	            "confidence_abs_error",
498	        ):
499	            if name in model_metrics:
500	                metrics[name] = model_metrics[name]
501	        return StepOutput(
502	            loss=loss,
503	            metrics=metrics,
504	            ratio_metrics=ratio_metrics,
505	        )
506	
507	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
508	        return {
509	            k.replace("draft_model.", ""): v
510	            for k, v in state_dict.items()
511	            if "draft_model." in k
512	        }
513	
514	
515	class DominoTrainStrategy(DraftTrainStrategy):
516	    """Domino block-parallel strategy wrapping ``OnlineDominoModel``.
517	
518	    Shares the trainer/backend/loader/checkpoint spine and feature schema with
519	    DFlash. Unlike the others, its loss blends a base loss with a weight
520	    ``lambda_base`` that decays over training, so it reads :class:`StepContext`.
521	    """
522	
523	    name = "domino"
524	    required_features = {"input_ids", "hidden_states", "loss_mask"}
525	
526	    def __init__(
527	        self,
528	        domino_model: nn.Module,
529	        *,
530	        lambda_start: float = 1.0,
531	        decay_ratio: float = 0.5,
532	    ) -> None:
533	        self.domino_model = domino_model
534	        self.lambda_start = lambda_start
535	        self.decay_ratio = decay_ratio
536	
537	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:
538	        return self.domino_model
539	
540	    def _device(self) -> torch.device:
541	        return next(self.domino_model.parameters()).device
542	
543	    def _lambda_base(self, ctx: Optional[StepContext]) -> float:
544	        # No schedule horizon -> pure final loss (lambda_base = 0).
545	        if ctx is None or not ctx.total_steps:
546	            return 0.0
547	        return linear_lambda_base(
548	            ctx.global_step, ctx.total_steps, self.lambda_start, self.decay_ratio
549	        )
550	
551	    def forward_loss(
552	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
553	    ) -> StepOutput:
554	        self.validate_batch(batch)
555	        t = batch.tensors
556	        device = self._device()
557	        lambda_base = self._lambda_base(ctx)
558	        loss, accuracy, model_metrics = self.domino_model(
559	            input_ids=t["input_ids"].to(device),
560	            hidden_states=t["hidden_states"].to(device),
561	            loss_mask=t["loss_mask"].to(device),
562	            lambda_base=lambda_base,
563	        )
564	        metrics = dict(model_metrics)
565	        metrics["accuracy"] = accuracy.detach()
566	        metrics.setdefault(
567	            "lambda_base",
568	            torch.tensor(float(lambda_base), device=loss.device),
569	        )
570	        return StepOutput(loss=loss, metrics=metrics)
571	
572	    def checkpoint_state_filter(self, state_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
573	        # Everything trainable lives under draft_model.; the target
574	        # embedding/head are a separate module, not persisted as draft weights.
575	        return {
576	            k.replace("draft_model.", ""): v
577	            for k, v in state_dict.items()
578	            if "draft_model." in k
579	        }
580	
581	
582	__all__ = [
583	    "DraftTrainStrategy",
584	    "Eagle3TrainStrategy",
585	    "PEagleTrainStrategy",
586	    "DFlashTrainStrategy",
587	    "DSparkTrainStrategy",
588	    "DominoTrainStrategy",
589	    "StepOutput",
590	    "StepContext",
591	    "linear_lambda_base",
592	]

content.content:
1	"""Executable provider ports for built-in algorithms.
2	
3	The public :class:`~specforge.algorithms.contracts.AlgorithmSpec` deliberately
4	contains values only.  This module is the executable half of one
5	``AlgorithmRegistration``: step/model factories and data adapters selected by
6	the application composition root.
7	
8	The provider types describe algorithm-owned behavior, never deployment
9	topology.  In particular, ``ServerStreamingProvider`` means that an algorithm
10	can consume features captured by an external server; it does not select a
11	backend, start a server, or construct a transport.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import hashlib
17	from dataclasses import dataclass
18	from types import MappingProxyType
19	from typing import (
20	    Any,
21	    Callable,
22	    FrozenSet,
23	    Iterable,
24	    Mapping,
25	    Protocol,
26	    Sequence,
27	    Tuple,
28	    runtime_checkable,
29	)
30	
31	from specforge.algorithms.contracts import AlgorithmSpec, FeatureMode
32	from specforge.algorithms.registry import AlgorithmRegistration
33	
34	Factory = Callable[..., Any]
35	
36	STEP_OPTIONS_CONTRACT_KEY = "specforge_step_options"
37	MODEL_PROVENANCE_CONTRACT_KEY = "specforge_model_provenance"
38	OMITTED_STATE_FINGERPRINT_CONTRACT_KEY = (
39	    "specforge_omitted_checkpoint_state_fingerprint"
40	)
41	
42	
43	@runtime_checkable
44	class ServerInputAdapter(Protocol):
45	    """Modality-owned input port for external server capture.
46	
47	    Implementations may load a tokenizer, processor, or any other immutable
48	    input tooling; turn configured source data into JSON-safe prompt payloads;
49	    and map a batch of those payloads onto the SGLang ``/generate`` request.
50	    The transport owns capture metadata and sampling parameters, so adapters
51	    return only model-input fields.
52	    """
53	
54	    def load_input_tools(self, config: Any) -> Any:
55	        """Load tokenizer/processor tooling required by this modality."""
56	
57	    def prepare_prompts(
58	        self,
59	        config: Any,
60	        input_tools: Any,
61	        *,
62	        draft_config: Any,
63	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
64	        """Build JSON-safe prompt dictionaries for the producer runtime."""
65	
66	    def build_request_inputs(
67	        self,
68	        tasks: Sequence[Any],
69	    ) -> Mapping[str, Any]:
70	        """Build model-input fields for one batched server request."""
71	
72	
73	def _non_empty(value: str, *, field_name: str) -> str:
74	    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value or value.strip() != value:
75	        raise ValueError(
76	            f"{field_name} must be a non-empty name without surrounding whitespace"
77	        )
78	    return value
79	
80	
81	def _factories_are_callable(values: Iterable[tuple[str, object]]) -> None:
82	    for field_name, value in values:
83	        if not callable(value):
84	            raise TypeError(f"{field_name} must be callable")
85	
86	
87	def checkpoint_key_fingerprint(model: Any, keys: Iterable[str]) -> str:
88	    """Hash selected state tensors with bounded host memory.
89	
90	    Providers may intentionally omit reconstructable frozen tensors from a
91	    checkpoint. Their values still affect the model, so resume records this
92	    fingerprint and rejects a reconstruction from different weights.
93	    """
94	
95	    import torch
96	
97	    state = model.state_dict()
98	    hasher = hashlib.sha256()
99	    for key in sorted(keys):
100	        if key not in state:
101	            raise ValueError(
102	                f"cannot fingerprint missing checkpoint-policy key {key!r}"
103	            )
104	        tensor = state[key]
105	        if not torch.is_tensor(tensor):
106	            raise TypeError(
107	                f"checkpoint-policy key {key!r} is not a tensor: "
108	                f"{type(tensor).__name__}"
109	            )
110	        hasher.update(key.encode("utf-8"))
111	        hasher.update(str(tensor.dtype).encode("ascii"))
112	        hasher.update(repr(tuple(tensor.shape)).encode("ascii"))
113	        flat = tensor.detach().contiguous().view(-1)
114	        chunk_elements = max(1, (16 * 1024 * 1024) // flat.element_size())
115	        for start in range(0, flat.numel(), chunk_elements):
116	            chunk = flat[start : start + chunk_elements].to(
117	                device="cpu",
118	                copy=True,
119	            )
120	            hasher.update(memoryview(chunk.view(torch.uint8).numpy()))
121	    return hasher.hexdigest()
122

181	@dataclass(frozen=True)
182	class StepRuntimeConfig(Mapping[str, Any]):
183	    """Resolved step options and checkpoint policy for one training run.
184	
185	    The mapping interface exposes only strategy constructor options, so the
186	    existing runtime builders can forward this object through their
187	    ``strategy_kwargs`` seam. ``Trainer`` additionally reads the immutable
188	    checkpoint metadata carried alongside those options.
189	    """
190	
191	    options: Mapping[str, Any]
192	    resume_contract: Mapping[str, Any]
193	    allowed_missing_checkpoint_keys: FrozenSet[str]
194	
195	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
196	        options = dict(self.options)
197	        invalid_option_keys = sorted(
198	            repr(key) for key in options if not isinstance(key, str) or not key.strip()
199	        )
200	        if invalid_option_keys:
201	            raise ValueError(
202	                "step option keys must be non-empty strings, got "
203	                f"{invalid_option_keys}"
204	            )
205	        resume_contract = dict(self.resume_contract)
206	        invalid_contract_keys = sorted(
207	            repr(key)
208	            for key in resume_contract
209	            if not isinstance(key, str) or not key.strip()
210	        )
211	        if invalid_contract_keys:
212	            raise ValueError(
213	                "resume_contract keys must be non-empty strings, got "
214	                f"{invalid_contract_keys}"
215	            )
216	        expected_options_contract = tuple(
217	            (key, options[key]) for key in sorted(options)
218	        )
219	        recorded_options_contract = resume_contract.setdefault(
220	            STEP_OPTIONS_CONTRACT_KEY,
221	            expected_options_contract,
222	        )
223	        if recorded_options_contract != expected_options_contract:
224	            raise ValueError(
225	                f"{STEP_OPTIONS_CONTRACT_KEY!r} must exactly match the resolved "
226	                f"step options: expected {expected_options_contract!r}, got "
227	                f"{recorded_options_contract!r}"
228	            )
229	        allowed_missing = frozenset(self.allowed_missing_checkpoint_keys)
230	        invalid_missing_keys = sorted(
231	            repr(key)
232	            for key in allowed_missing
233	            if not isinstance(key, str) or not key.strip()
234	        )
235	        if invalid_missing_keys:
236	            raise ValueError(
237	                "allowed missing checkpoint keys must be non-empty strings, got "
238	                f"{invalid_missing_keys}"
239	            )
240	        if (
241	            allowed_missing
242	            and OMITTED_STATE_FINGERPRINT_CONTRACT_KEY not in resume_contract
243	        ):
244	            raise ValueError(
245	                "allowed missing checkpoint keys require the runtime-owned "
246	                f"{OMITTED_STATE_FINGERPRINT_CONTRACT_KEY!r} resume contract"
247	            )
248	        object.__setattr__(self, "options", MappingProxyType(options))
249	        object.__setattr__(
250	            self,
251	            "resume_contract",
252	            MappingProxyType(resume_contract),
253	        )
254	        object.__setattr__(self, "allowed_missing_checkpoint_keys", allowed_missing)
255	
256	    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
257	        return self.options[key]
258	
259	    def __iter__(self):
260	        return iter(self.options)
261	
262	    def __len__(self) -> int:
263	        return len(self.options)
264

266	@dataclass(frozen=True)
267	class StepProvider:
268	    """Factories and persistence hooks for one algorithm's training step."""
269	
270	    build: Factory
271	    options: Factory
272	    resume_contract: Factory
273	    allowed_missing_checkpoint_keys: Factory
274	    uses_external_target_head: bool
275	
276	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
277	        _factories_are_callable(
278	            (
279	                ("build", self.build),
280	                ("options", self.options),
281	                ("resume_contract", self.resume_contract),
282	                (
283	                    "allowed_missing_checkpoint_keys",
284	                    self.allowed_missing_checkpoint_keys,
285	                ),
286	            )
287	        )
288	        if not isinstance(self.uses_external_target_head, bool):
289	            raise TypeError("uses_external_target_head must be a bool")
290	
291	    def bind_runtime(
292	        self,
293	        config: Any,
294	        draft_model: Any,
295	        training_model: Any,
296	        *,
297	        model_provenance: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
298	    ) -> StepRuntimeConfig:
299	        """Resolve algorithm checkpoint semantics against the live model."""
300	
301	        options = self.options(config)
302	        if not isinstance(options, Mapping):
303	            raise TypeError(
304	                "step options must return a mapping, got " f"{type(options).__name__}"
305	            )
306	        provider_contract = self.resume_contract(config, draft_model, training_model)
307	        if not isinstance(provider_contract, Mapping):
308	            raise TypeError(
309	                "step resume_contract must return a mapping, got "
310	                f"{type(provider_contract).__name__}"
311	            )
312	        resume_contract = dict(provider_contract)
313	        reserved = {
314	            STEP_OPTIONS_CONTRACT_KEY,
315	            MODEL_PROVENANCE_CONTRACT_KEY,
316	            OMITTED_STATE_FINGERPRINT_CONTRACT_KEY,
317	        } & resume_contract.keys()
318	        if reserved:
319	            raise ValueError(
320	                "provider resume_contract uses runtime-owned fields: "
321	                f"{sorted(reserved)}"
322	            )
323	        resume_contract[STEP_OPTIONS_CONTRACT_KEY] = tuple(
324	            (key, options[key]) for key in sorted(options)
325	        )
326	        if model_provenance is not None:
327	            resume_contract[MODEL_PROVENANCE_CONTRACT_KEY] = tuple(
328	                (key, model_provenance[key]) for key in sorted(model_provenance)
329	            )
330	        allowed_missing = frozenset(
331	            self.allowed_missing_checkpoint_keys(
332	                config,
333	                draft_model,
334	                training_model,
335	            )
336	        )
337	        if allowed_missing:
338	            resume_contract[OMITTED_STATE_FINGERPRINT_CONTRACT_KEY] = (
339	                checkpoint_key_fingerprint(draft_model, allowed_missing)
340	            )
341	        return StepRuntimeConfig(
342	            options=options,
343	            resume_contract=resume_contract,
344	            allowed_missing_checkpoint_keys=allowed_missing,
345	        )

389	@dataclass(frozen=True)
390	class OfflineCaptureLayout:
391	    """Map one generic local capture onto the persisted offline schema.
392	
393	    Local capture always exposes the same four per-sample sources:
394	    ``input_ids``, ``loss_mask``, ``aux_hidden_states``, and
395	    ``last_hidden_states``. Algorithms own the names they persist so the data
396	    preparation command cannot silently drift away from their storage
397	    contracts.
398	    """
399	
400	    capture_method: str
401	    aux_feature: str | None
402	    last_hidden_feature: str | None
403	    passthrough: Tuple[Tuple[str, str], ...]
404	
405	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
406	        _non_empty(self.capture_method, field_name="capture_method")
407	        for field_name in ("aux_feature", "last_hidden_feature"):
408	            value = getattr(self, field_name)
409	            if value is not None:
410	                _non_empty(value, field_name=field_name)
411	
412	        passthrough = tuple(self.passthrough)
413	        for feature_name, source_key in passthrough:
414	            _non_empty(feature_name, field_name="passthrough feature name")
415	            _non_empty(source_key, field_name="passthrough source key")
416	        object.__setattr__(self, "passthrough", passthrough)
417	
418	        output_names = self.output_names
419	        duplicates = sorted(
420	            name for name in set(output_names) if output_names.count(name) > 1
421	        )
422	        if duplicates:
423	            raise ValueError(
424	                f"duplicate offline capture output feature names: {duplicates}"
425	            )
426	        if not output_names:
427	            raise ValueError("offline capture layout must emit at least one feature")
428	
429	    @property
430	    def output_names(self) -> Tuple[str, ...]:
431	        """Return persisted feature names in deterministic layout order."""
432	
433	        names = [feature for feature, _source in self.passthrough]
434	        if self.aux_feature is not None:
435	            names.append(self.aux_feature)
436	        if self.last_hidden_feature is not None:
437	            names.append(self.last_hidden_feature)
438	        return tuple(names)
439	
440	    def materialize(self, sources: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
441	        """Build one persisted record and fail early on missing capture data."""
442	
443	        mappings = list(self.passthrough)
444	        if self.aux_feature is not None:
445	            mappings.append((self.aux_feature, "aux_hidden_states"))
446	        if self.last_hidden_feature is not None:
447	            mappings.append((self.last_hidden_feature, "last_hidden_states"))
448	
449	        record = {}
450	        for feature_name, source_key in mappings:
451	            if source_key not in sources:
452	                raise KeyError(
453	                    f"offline capture source {source_key!r} required for "
454	                    f"feature {feature_name!r} is missing"
455	                )
456	            value = sources[source_key]
457	            if value is None:
458	                raise ValueError(
459	                    f"offline capture source {source_key!r} required for "
460	                    f"feature {feature_name!r} is None"
461	                )
462	            record[feature_name] = value
463	        return record
464	
465

531	    can leave it unset; the current runtime does not support VLM registration
532	    or media requests.

560	        """Construct and validate the optional modality-owned input adapter."""
561

565	        required = (

569	        )

571	            name for name in required if not callable(getattr(adapter, name, None))
572	        ]
573	        if missing:

581	@dataclass(frozen=True)
582	class AlgorithmProviders:
583	    """Executable providers paired with one pure ``AlgorithmSpec``."""
584	
585	    algorithm_name: str
586	    step: StepProvider
587	    model: ModelProvider
588	    offline: Tuple[OfflineDataProvider, ...] = ()
589	    server_streaming: Tuple[ServerStreamingProvider, ...] = ()
590	    vocab_mapping_modes: FrozenSet[FeatureMode] = frozenset()
591	
592	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
593	        _non_empty(self.algorithm_name, field_name="algorithm_name")
594	        if not isinstance(self.step, StepProvider):
595	            raise TypeError("step must be a StepProvider")
596	        if not isinstance(self.model, ModelProvider):
597	            raise TypeError("model must be a ModelProvider")
598	        offline = tuple(self.offline)
599	        streaming = tuple(self.server_streaming)
600	        self._validate_unique_modalities(offline, field_name="offline")
601	        self._validate_unique_modalities(
602	            streaming,
603	            field_name="server_streaming",
604	        )
605	        modes = frozenset(FeatureMode(mode) for mode in self.vocab_mapping_modes)
606	        object.__setattr__(self, "offline", offline)
607	        object.__setattr__(self, "server_streaming", streaming)
608	        object.__setattr__(self, "vocab_mapping_modes", modes)
609	
610	    @staticmethod
611	    def _validate_unique_modalities(providers, *, field_name: str) -> None:
612	        modalities = [provider.modality for provider in providers]
613	        duplicates = sorted(
614	            modality for modality in set(modalities) if modalities.count(modality) > 1
615	        )
616	        if duplicates:
617	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate {field_name} modalities: {duplicates}")
618	
619	    def offline_for(self, modality: str) -> OfflineDataProvider:
620	        return self._provider_for(self.offline, modality, kind="offline")
621	
622	    def server_streaming_for(self, modality: str) -> ServerStreamingProvider:
623	        return self._provider_for(
624	            self.server_streaming,
625	            modality,
626	            kind="server streaming",
627	        )
628	
629	    def _provider_for(self, providers, modality: str, *, kind: str):
630	        for provider in providers:
631	            if provider.modality == modality:
632	                return provider
633	        available = sorted(provider.modality for provider in providers)
634	        raise KeyError(
635	            f"algorithm {self.algorithm_name!r} has no {kind} provider for "
636	            f"modality {modality!r}; available: {available}"
637	        )
638	
639	
640	def make_registration(
641	    spec: AlgorithmSpec,
642	    providers: AlgorithmProviders,
643	) -> AlgorithmRegistration:
644	    """Validate contract/provider parity and return one registration."""
645	
646	    if providers.algorithm_name != spec.name:
647	        raise ValueError(
648	            f"provider algorithm {providers.algorithm_name!r} does not match "
649	            f"spec {spec.name!r}"
650	        )
651	    if providers.model.draft_config.architecture not in (
652	        spec.draft.compatible_architectures
653	    ):
654	        raise ValueError(
655	            "model provider architecture is not compatible with the algorithm "
656	            f"contract: {providers.model.draft_config.architecture!r}"
657	        )
658	
659	    expected = {contract.key for contract in spec.feature_contracts}
660	    provided = {
661	        *((FeatureMode.OFFLINE, provider.modality) for provider in providers.offline),
662	        *(
663	            (FeatureMode.STREAMING, provider.modality)
664	            for provider in providers.server_streaming
665	        ),
666	    }
667	    if provided != expected:
668	        raise ValueError(
669	            "feature contract/provider keys differ: "
670	            f"contracts={sorted((mode.value, modality) for mode, modality in expected)}, "
671	            f"providers={sorted((mode.value, modality) for mode, modality in provided)}"
672	        )
673	    has_vocab_mapping_provider = bool(providers.vocab_mapping_modes)
674	    if has_vocab_mapping_provider != spec.capabilities.supports_vocab_mapping:
675	        raise ValueError(
676	            "vocab-mapping capability/provider mismatch: "
677	            f"contract={spec.capabilities.supports_vocab_mapping}, "
678	            f"provider_modes={sorted(mode.value for mode in providers.vocab_mapping_modes)}"
679	        )
680	    unavailable_mapping_modes = providers.vocab_mapping_modes - spec.feature_modes
681	    if unavailable_mapping_modes:
682	        raise ValueError(
683	            "vocab-mapping provider modes lack feature contracts: "
684	            f"{sorted(mode.value for mode in unavailable_mapping_modes)}"
685	        )
686	
687	    for provider in providers.offline:
688	        contract = spec.feature_contract(FeatureMode.OFFLINE, provider.modality)
689	        if contract.storage is None:  # pragma: no cover - contract invariant
690	            raise ValueError("offline contract is missing storage")
691	        if provider.normalizer_id != contract.storage.normalizer:
692	            raise ValueError(
693	                f"offline normalizer mismatch for {provider.modality!r}: "
694	                f"{provider.normalizer_id!r} != {contract.storage.normalizer!r}"
695	            )
696	        if provider.capture_layout is not None:
697	            emitted = set(provider.capture_layout.output_names)
698	            required = set(contract.storage.required_tensors)
699	            if emitted != required:
700	                raise ValueError(
701	                    f"offline capture layout for {provider.modality!r} must "
702	                    "exactly match the storage contract: "
703	                    f"emitted={sorted(emitted)}, required={sorted(required)}"
704	                )
705	            streaming = next(
706	                (
707	                    candidate
708	                    for candidate in providers.server_streaming
709	                    if candidate.modality == provider.modality
710	                ),
711	                None,
712	            )
713	            if (
714	                streaming is not None
715	                and provider.capture_layout.capture_method != streaming.capture_method
716	            ):
717	                raise ValueError(
718	                    f"offline and server capture methods differ for "
719	                    f"{provider.modality!r}: "
720	                    f"{provider.capture_layout.capture_method!r} != "
721	                    f"{streaming.capture_method!r}"
722	                )
723	    for provider in providers.server_streaming:
724	        contract = spec.feature_contract(FeatureMode.STREAMING, provider.modality)
725	        if provider.target_representation != contract.default_target_representation:
726	            raise ValueError(
727	                f"streaming target representation mismatch for "
728	                f"{provider.modality!r}: {provider.target_representation!r} != "
729	                f"{contract.default_target_representation!r}"
730	            )
731	        layout = provider.layout
732	        emitted = {
733	            *(
734	                feature
735	                for feature in (
736	                    layout.aux_feature,
737	                    layout.last_hidden_feature,
738	                    layout.attention_mask_feature,
739	                )
740	                if feature is not None
741	            ),
742	            *(feature for feature, _payload, _shape in layout.passthrough),
743	        }
744	        missing = contract.required_tensors - emitted
745	        if missing:
746	            raise ValueError(
747	                f"server capture layout for {provider.modality!r} does not emit "
748	                f"required tensors: {sorted(missing)}"
749	            )
750	
751	    return AlgorithmRegistration(spec=spec, providers=providers)
752	
753	
754	__all__ = [
755	    "AlgorithmProviders",
756	    "DraftConfigProvider",
757	    "MODEL_PROVENANCE_CONTRACT_KEY",
758	    "ModelProvider",
759	    "OMITTED_STATE_FINGERPRINT_CONTRACT_KEY",

1	"""Explicit, immutable registrations for algorithm contracts and providers."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import Iterable, Iterator, Tuple
7	
8	from specforge.algorithms.contracts import AlgorithmSpec
9	
10	
11	@dataclass(frozen=True)
12	class AlgorithmRegistration:
13	    """One lookup result containing pure metadata and executable providers.
14	
15	    Keeping the two halves in one registration avoids parallel spec/provider
16	    registries.  Planning reads ``spec``; the composition root alone reads
17	    ``providers``.
18	    """
19	
20	    spec: AlgorithmSpec
21	    providers: object
22	
23	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
24	        if not isinstance(self.spec, AlgorithmSpec):
25	            raise TypeError("spec must be an AlgorithmSpec")
26	        if self.providers is None:
27	            raise TypeError("providers must not be None")
28	        provider_name = getattr(self.providers, "algorithm_name", self.spec.name)
29	        if provider_name != self.spec.name:
30	            raise ValueError(
31	                "provider algorithm_name must match spec.name: "
32	                f"{provider_name!r} != {self.spec.name!r}"
33	            )
34	
35	    @property
36	    def name(self) -> str:
37	        return self.spec.name
38	
39	
40	@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
41	class AlgorithmRegistry:
42	    """Immutable catalog assembled explicitly by the composition root."""
43	
44	    _registrations: Tuple[AlgorithmRegistration, ...]
45	
46	    def __init__(
47	        self,
48	        registrations: Iterable[AlgorithmRegistration] = (),
49	    ) -> None:
50	        resolved = tuple(registrations)
51	        invalid = [
52	            type(registration).__name__
53	            for registration in resolved
54	            if not isinstance(registration, AlgorithmRegistration)
55	        ]
56	        if invalid:
57	            raise TypeError(
58	                "registry values must be AlgorithmRegistration, got " f"{invalid}"
59	            )
60	        names = [registration.name for registration in resolved]
61	        duplicates = sorted(name for name in set(names) if names.count(name) > 1)
62	        if duplicates:
63	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate algorithm registrations: {duplicates}")
64	        object.__setattr__(
65	            self,
66	            "_registrations",
67	            tuple(sorted(resolved, key=lambda registration: registration.name)),
68	        )
69	
70	    @property
71	    def names(self) -> Tuple[str, ...]:
72	        return tuple(registration.name for registration in self._registrations)
73	
74	    @property
75	    def registrations(self) -> Tuple[AlgorithmRegistration, ...]:
76	        return self._registrations
77	
78	    def resolve(self, name: str) -> AlgorithmRegistration:
79	        for registration in self._registrations:
80	            if registration.name == name:
81	                return registration
82	        raise KeyError(
83	            f"unknown algorithm {name!r}; registered algorithms: {list(self.names)}"
84	        )
85	
86	    def with_registration(
87	        self,
88	        registration: AlgorithmRegistration,
89	    ) -> "AlgorithmRegistry":
90	        return AlgorithmRegistry((*self._registrations, registration))
91	
92	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[AlgorithmRegistration]:
93	        return iter(self._registrations)
94	
95	    def __len__(self) -> int:
96	        return len(self._registrations)
97	
98	
99	__all__ = ["AlgorithmRegistration", "AlgorithmRegistry"]
100

229	            ),
230	        ),
231	        vocab_mapping_modes=frozenset({FeatureMode.OFFLINE, FeatureMode.STREAMING}),
232	    )
233	
234	
235	def create_registration():
236	    return make_registration(algorithm_spec(), algorithm_providers())
237	
238	
239	__all__ = ["algorithm_providers", "algorithm_spec", "create_registration"]

"""Shared DFlash-family normalization and padding adapters."""

from __future__ import annotations

from functools import partial

from specforge.algorithms.common.collation import pad_and_concatenate_features

NORMALIZER_ID = "dflash_family_offline_v1"
DSPARK_NORMALIZER_ID = "dspark_offline_v1"


def _normalize_hidden_states(
    raw,
    key: str,
    max_len: int,
    *,
    description: str,
):
    hidden_states = raw[key]
    if hidden_states.dim() == 3:
        if hidden_states.shape[0] != 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"offline {description} must have shape [seq, width] or "
                f"[1, seq, width], got {tuple(hidden_states.shape)}"
            )
        hidden_states = hidden_states.squeeze(0)
    if hidden_states.dim() != 2:
        raise ValueError(
            f"offline {description} must have shape [seq, width] or "
            f"[1, seq, width], got {tuple(hidden_states.shape)}"
        )
    return hidden_states[:max_len].unsqueeze(0)


def normalize_offline_sample(raw, max_len: int):
    """Normalize raw DFlash/Domino capture tensors without target projection."""

    input_ids = raw["input_ids"][:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
    loss_mask = raw["loss_mask"][:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
    hidden_states = _normalize_hidden_states(
        raw,
        "hidden_states",
        max_len,
        description="DFlash-family hidden_states",
    )
    lengths = {
        input_ids.shape[1],
        loss_mask.shape[1],
        hidden_states.shape[1],
    }
    if len(lengths) != 1:
        raise ValueError(
            "offline DFlash-family features have mismatched sequence lengths "
            f"after truncation: input_ids={input_ids.shape[1]}, "
            f"loss_mask={loss_mask.shape[1]}, "
            f"hidden_states={hidden_states.shape[1]}"
        )
    return {
        "input_ids": input_ids,
        "loss_mask": loss_mask,
        "hidden_states": hidden_states,
    }


def normalize_dspark_offline_sample(raw, max_len: int):
    """Normalize DSpark capture tensors, including target final-layer states."""

    normalized = normalize_offline_sample(raw, max_len)
    target_last_hidden_states = _normalize_hidden_states(
        raw,
        "target_last_hidden_states",
        max_len,
        description="DSpark target_last_hidden_states",
    )
    expected_length = normalized["input_ids"].shape[1]
    if target_last_hidden_states.shape[1] != expected_length:
        raise ValueError(
            "offline DSpark features have mismatched sequence lengths after "
            f"truncation: input_ids={expected_length}, "
            "target_last_hidden_states="
            f"{target_last_hidden_states.shape[1]}"
        )
    return {
        **normalized,
        "target_last_hidden_states": target_last_hidden_states,
    }


def build_offline_reader(
    strategy,
    hidden_states_path,
    *,
    run_id,
    ttt_length,
    max_len,
):
    # Transitional runtime import; the composition root will inject this port.
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.offline_reader import OfflineManifestReader

    return OfflineManifestReader(
        hidden_states_path,
        run_id=run_id,
        strategy=strategy,
        feature_keys=("input_ids", "loss_mask", "hidden_states"),
        target_repr=None,
        ttt_length=ttt_length,
        max_len=max_len,
    )


def build_dspark_offline_reader(
    strategy,
    hidden_states_path,
    *,
    run_id,
    ttt_length,
    max_len,
):
    # Transitional runtime import; the composition root will inject this port.
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.offline_reader import OfflineManifestReader

    return OfflineManifestReader(
        hidden_states_path,
        run_id=run_id,
        strategy=strategy,
        feature_keys=(
            "input_ids",
            "loss_mask",
            "hidden_states",
            "target_last_hidden_states",
        ),
        target_repr="hidden_state",
        ttt_length=ttt_length,
        max_len=max_len,
    )


def build_offline_normalizer(max_len, **_topology):
    return partial(normalize_offline_sample, max_len=max_len)


def build_dspark_offline_normalizer(max_len, **_topology):
    return partial(normalize_dspark_offline_sample, max_len=max_len)


def build_collator():
    def collate(features):
        return pad_and_concatenate_features(
            features,
            sequence_axes={
                "input_ids": 1,
                "loss_mask": 1,
                "hidden_states": 1,
            },
            required_keys=("input_ids", "loss_mask", "hidden_states"),
        )

    return collate


def build_dspark_collator():
    def collate(features):
        return pad_and_concatenate_features(
            features,
            sequence_axes={
                "input_ids": 1,
                "loss_mask": 1,
                "hidden_states": 1,
                "target_last_hidden_states": 1,
            },
            required_keys=(
                "input_ids",
                "loss_mask",
                "hidden_states",
                "target_last_hidden_states",
            ),
        )

    return collate


__all__ = [
    "DSPARK_NORMALIZER_ID",
    "NORMALIZER_ID",
    "build_collator",
    "build_dspark_collator",
    "build_dspark_offline_normalizer",
    "build_dspark_offline_reader",
    "build_offline_normalizer",
    "build_offline_reader",
    "normalize_dspark_offline_sample",
    "normalize_offline_sample",
]

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
"""Algorithm-owned model and configuration providers.

The public assembler owns topology and lifecycle.  This module owns the facts
that vary by draft method: how to create its config and model, which target
features it captures, and which dataset policies it needs.  All heavy imports
remain inside callables so resolving the algorithm registry stays import-light.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from transformers import PretrainedConfig

    from specforge.config import Config
    from specforge.modeling.draft.dflash_kernels import DFlashKernels


@dataclass
class AlgorithmModelParts:
    """Algorithm-specific pieces returned to the application assembler."""

    model: Any
    target_head: Any = None
    capture_layers: Optional[List[int]] = None


def dflash_needs_input_tools(cfg: Config, draft_model: Any) -> bool:
    method_config = getattr(draft_model.config, "dflash_config", None) or {}
    needs_mask_fallback = (
        cfg.model.mask_token_id is None and method_config.get("mask_token_id") is None
    )
    return cfg.mode == "online" or needs_mask_fallback


def _torch_dtype(cfg: Config):
    import torch

    return getattr(torch, cfg.model.torch_dtype)


def _device():
    from specforge.utils import get_local_device

content.content:
1	# Customize a training run
2	
3	Customization starts from a typed YAML config. Pick the closest checked-in
4	file under
5	[`examples/configs`](https://github.com/sgl-project/SpecForge/tree/main/examples/configs),
6	change model and data paths, and keep the same training entry point:
7	
8	```bash
9	specforge train --config ./my-run.yaml
10	```
11	
12	For one-off changes, use dotted overrides rather than adding another launcher:
13	
14	```bash
15	specforge train \
16	  --config examples/configs/qwen3-8b-eagle3-disaggregated.yaml \
17	  model.target_model_path=/models/my-target \
18	  data.train_data_path=/datasets/my-training-data.jsonl \
19	  training.learning_rate=5e-5
20	```
21	
22	The config is strict: unknown fields, invalid strategy/topology combinations,
23	and unsupported attention backends are errors. See
24	[`specforge/config/schema.py`](../../specforge/config/schema.py) and the
25	[training guide](../basic_usage/training.md) for the accepted fields.
26	
27	## Chat templates
28	
29	Register a text chat template in `TEMPLATE_REGISTRY` in
30	`specforge/data/template.py`, then reference its name with
31	`data.chat_template`:
32	
33	```python
34	TEMPLATE_REGISTRY.register(
35	    name="your-template-name",
36	    template=ChatTemplate(
37	        assistant_header="...",
38	        user_header="...",
39	        system_prompt="...",
40	        end_of_turn_token="...",
41	    ),
42	)
43	```
44	
45	```yaml
46	data:
47	  train_data_path: /datasets/my-training-data.jsonl
48	  chat_template: your-template-name
49	  max_length: 4096
50	```
51	
52	The server capture runtime accepts text input only. VLM training, including
53	`qwen2_5_vl`, is not supported. Attention backends are a closed,
54	strategy-specific set:
55	
56	| Strategy | Accepted `training.attention_backend` values |
57	| --- | --- |
58	| EAGLE3 | `sdpa`, `flex_attention`, `fa`, offline `usp` |
59	| P-EAGLE | `flex_attention` |
60	| DFlash, Domino, DSpark | `eager`, `sdpa`, `flex_attention` |
61	
62	## Target models
63	
64	For a Hugging Face-compatible target, normally only these model fields need to
65	change:
66	
67	```yaml
68	model:
69	  target_model_path: organization/model-name
70	  target_backend: sglang
71	  trust_remote_code: false
72	  embedding_key: model.embed_tokens.weight
73	  lm_head_key: lm_head.weight
74	```
75	
76	Every online run uses `model.target_backend: sglang`. Add target-model support
77	to the SGLang capture server instead of adding an HF/custom target loader to
78	the trainer. Target TP/EP and model-specific inference stay on that server;
79	the SpecForge consumer receives only the algorithm's versioned feature schema.
80	Offline feature training performs no target inference.
81	
82	EAGLE3 offline sequence parallelism is selected with
83	`training.attention_backend: usp` plus `training.sp_ulysses_size` and
84	`training.sp_ring_size`. Evaluation, compact-teacher projection, and experiment
85	tracking are also config features rather than custom launchers; see the
86	[training guide](../basic_usage/training.md) for their validated combinations.
87	
88	## Draft architectures
89	
90	Draft classes register through `@register_draft`. The key defaults to the
91	class name and must match the single entry in the draft JSON's
92	`architectures` list:
93	
94	```python
95	from transformers import PretrainedConfig
96	
97	from specforge.modeling.draft.base import Eagle3DraftModel
98	from specforge.modeling.draft.registry import register_draft
99	
100	
101	class MyDraftConfig(PretrainedConfig):
102	    model_type = "my-draft"
103	
104	
105	@register_draft
106	class MyEagle3Draft(Eagle3DraftModel):
107	    config_class = MyDraftConfig
108	
109	    def __init__(self, config, **kwargs):
110	        super().__init__(config)
111	        ...
112	```
113	
114	Import the module from `specforge/modeling/draft/__init__.py` so registration
115	runs before config resolution. A minimal draft config then contains:
116	
117	```json
118	{
